system.description: The caster sends a hideous and unsettling phantasmal vision to a single specific creature whom the caster names or specifically designates. The nightmare prevents restful sleep and causes 1d10 points of damage. The nightmare leaves the subject tired and unrested, and unable to regain arcane spells for the next 24 hours. Dispel evil cast on the subject while the caster is casting the spell dispels the nightmare and stuns the caster for 10 minutes per caster level of the dispel evil. If the recipient is awake when the spell begins, the caster can choose to cease casting (ending the spell) or enter a trance until the recipient goes to sleep, whereupon the caster becomes alert again and completes the casting. If the caster is disturbed during the trance, the spell ends. If the caster chooses to enter a trance, the caster is not aware of the caster's surroundings or the activities around the caster while in the trance. The caster is defenseless, both physically and mentally, while in the trance. Creatures who don't sleep or dream are immune to this spell.
